name: Derby Sage Parsnips (English)
recipeIngredient: 1 1/2 oz Derby sage cheese, finely grated
recipeInstructions:
1. Set oven to 400°F or Mark 6.
2. Mix the flour, cheese, salt, and pepper in a mixing bowl.
3. Peel the parsnips and cut into small chunks.
4. Place the parsnips in a pan of boiling, salted water and bring back to the boil.
5. Cover and cook for 3 minutes.
6. Drain the parsnips and, while hot, drop a few at a time into the cheese mixture.
7. Shake the bowl to ensure an even coating, then transfer to a tray.
8. Place the dripping in a roasting tin and heat in the oven.
9. When hot, remove the tin from the oven and place it on the coated parsnips.
10. Spoon the hot fat over the parsnips, return to the oven, and bake for 35-40 minutes, turning over after 20 minutes.
11. The cheese mixture will only coat the parsnips while they are steaming. To keep them `steamy', drain only a few at a time.
